_description: Expected output for session-fixture.jsonl acceptance verification
_note: Run the script with --fixture scripts/fixtures/session-fixture.jsonl and diff dataset.json against the 'episodes' array here (ignoring episode_id which is a random UUID)

episodes._description: Episode 1: tasks:tasks — explicit trigger via Skill tool; includes a Read result whose content embeds the permission-denial magic phrase but is_error=false — must NOT count as a permission_denial. Negative case for the outcome detectors (no tests, no AskUserQuestion, no commit/PR).
Episode 2: complexity-review:complexity-review — rename alias -> project-quality; ambient trigger. Negative case for the outcome detectors.

Episode 3: github-releases:github-releases — explicit trigger. Positive case that fires EVERY outcome/friction detector branch so a regression that breaks one cannot ship green: a true permission denial (is_error + 'doesn't want to proceed'), an interruption (toolUseResult.interrupted), an AskUserQuestion, a pytest result (tests_run + tests_passed), and assistant text naming a commit and a pull request.

_design_notes: commit-commands episodes (4 turns) are filtered from main episode list — plugin not in marketplace.
unknown-plugin episode (1 turn) is also filtered.
Both appear in unmatched_plugins section of summary.md.
Episode 1: ls command ran twice with identical input => retries=1.
Episode 1: 'actually no, don't do that' text matches CORRECTION_RE => user_corrections=1.
Episode 1: is_error=true on bash failure tool_result => tool_errors=1.
Episode 1: Read tool_result (is_error=false) whose content contains "doesn't want to proceed" must NOT count as a permission_denial — this is the false-positive guard (see 1un).
Episode 1: the true permission denial (is_error=true + doesn't want to proceed) is in commit-commands episode, not here.
Episode 2: no Skill tool use before it => trigger_type=ambient.
Episode 2: complexity-review plugin resolved via rename alias to project-quality.
Episode 3: explicit trigger via same-turn Skill(github-releases:github-releases).
Episode 3: tool_errors=2 (the rejected git push AND the interrupted sleep both carry is_error=true).
Episode 3: permission_denials=1 (git push rejected with 'doesn't want to proceed', is_error=true).
Episode 3: ask_user_questions=1 (AskUserQuestion tool_use); tests_run+tests_passed from the pytest result; ended_in_commit+ended_in_pr from the final assistant text.
